title: Leptostelma tucumanense
taxonomy: Leptostelma tucumanense is a species of flowering plant in the family Boraginaceae. It is native to Argentina and is a member of the genus Leptostelma. Leptostelma tucumanense is a part of the subfamily Boraginoideae. The classification of Leptostelma tucumanense is further divided into order Lamiales and class Magnoliopsida. This classification reflects the plant's evolutionary relationships and characteristics.

intro: Leptostelma tucumanense is a species of flowering plant in the family Boraginaceae, commonly known as the Tucuman boraginaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Argentina and Uruguay, for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, as well as its ability to aid in digestion and reduce stress. Bioactive constituents of Leptostelma tucumanense include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Leptostelma tucumanense is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be consumed to promote overall well-being and alleviate symptoms of various health conditions.
